user_query: I need a small, reproducible prototype CLI that helps me collect and summarize official award information relevant to mechanical engineering, from authoritative organizations I respect. Use the provided configuration at input/queries.yaml and the output schema at input/schema.json. The tool must: 1) perform search engine queries from the Internet for each query in input/queries.yaml, 2) constrain selections to results from the allowed official domains listed per query, 3) download and cache the corresponding webpages locally under workspace/webcache, 4) extract a reasonable page title and attempt to parse a publication year when available, and 5) produce two outputs: a structured JSON at workspace/output/search_results.json that conforms to input/schema.json and a CSV aggregate at workspace/output/domain_stats.csv with counts per source_domain and organization. After producing these outputs, run a validation command that checks the JSON against input/schema.json, recomputes the domain counts from search_results.json, and verifies that they match the CSV. Save the validation log to workspace/reports/test_report.txt, and ensure the validation reports success. Requirements: - Internet use is required: perform actual search engine queries; do not hardcode URLs. - Only include results from the official domains specified for each query in input/queries.yaml. - Include at least three total entries across all queries; publication_year may be null if not reliably found. - Each JSON entry must include: query, result_url, source_domain, organization, item_title, publication_year (integer or null), retrieval_date (ISO-8601), and content_path pointing to the downloaded file under workspace/webcache. - domain_stats.csv must have columns: source_domain, organization, count. - Make the CLI executable via a command-line program or script; inspecting the provided configuration files should be essential to your implementation. - Run your search/download script and the validation command locally, and ensure the specified output paths are created. If validation fails, fix the outputs and rerun until workspace/reports/test_report.txt indicates success. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
